title: Local variables that never change should be declared const
messageFormat: Declare local variable '{0}' as const since it holds a constant value that never changes.
summary: A local initialised with a compile-time constant and never reassigned is a constant in everything but declaration. Marking it const records that fact and lets the compiler reject any later attempt to change it.
The reader then knows the value is fixed for the whole method without scanning for reassignments.
